spec.reasoning: The task instruction references columns by letter (Column A, Column B, Column C) and specific cell addresses (C6, A2, C2:C7), so column reorder and title_row_insert are risky due to the 'task_references_specific_cell_addresses' flag. Instead, I use safe perturbations: (1) Lexical rename of column headers — 'Name' to 'Department', 'Sub-Dept' to 'Sub-Division', 'Excel Formula' to 'Result Formula'. The instruction refers to 'name' and 'sub-dept' as conceptual labels but the column letter references (A, B, C) remain unchanged, so the task logic is preserved. The sheet is also renamed from 'Sheet1' to 'DataSheet'. (2) A distractor column 'Dept-Code' is added after the last column to test whether the model is confused by a plausibly related but irrelevant column. No data values need renaming since the data values like 'Sales', 'Sales-Marketing', and 'General' appear in the instruction body as meaningful English terms and must not be changed.
